title: linum flavum subsp. flavum
intro: Linum flavum subsp. flavum, commonly known as the yellow-flowering flax or linum flavum, is a subspecies of the flax plant (Linum flavum) native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and anti-arthritic properties, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Linum flavum subsp. flavum include reducing inflammation, improving skin conditions, and alleviating symptoms of arthritis and other joint-related issues. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, are commonly used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health complaints.
